description: Compile static scene layout, asset resolution, camera plan, and physics graph membership into deterministic runtime actor bindings for UE or another execution backend.
physical_assumptions: Runtime actors are created from stable case object ids, not from display names or prompt prose.
Physics-critical actors must preserve collider, mass, material, transform, and collision profile metadata.
Visual-only actors may be omitted from the physics graph but must remain traceable if rendered.
Camera bindings must reference planned views and deterministic transforms.
required_assets: UE package paths or analytic proxy actor definitions
verifier_rules: Every physics-critical object has exactly one runtime actor binding.
Runtime actor ids are unique and stable.
Actor bindings reference selected assets or explicit analytic proxies.
Collision graph object ids are present in runtime actor bindings.
Camera bindings cover every required view.
repair_suggestions: Run static scene placement before actor placement.
Update asset registry metadata for missing collider or material fields.
Use analytic proxy only when the selected real asset cannot satisfy physics-critical metadata.
Keep object ids stable from case spec through UE actor labels.
